Task.Name: Gamma
Phase 0 - Mobilization and confidentiality control
Task.Notes: Programme launch, restricted planning model, legal perimeter, and initial inventory for the Bosch Cloud carve-out into a 50/50 JV with Alibaba.

Task.Name: 0.1 Governance and restricted planning
Launch core programme team and initiate Gamma confidentiality controls
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Robert Bosch China IT + Alibaba IT | Only the core clean team is aware during startup because the JV and antitrust due diligence remain highly confidential.

Task.Name: Establish confidentiality protocol, clean-team access, and restricted stakeholder list
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Legal + Robert Bosch China IT Security | Planning access is tightly controlled until legal allows a broader discovery group.

Task.Name: Set governance cadence, RACI, steerco path, and PMO controls
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Robert Bosch China PMO + Alibaba PMO | Governance reflects shared management of the future 50/50 JV.

Task.Name: 0.2 Legal perimeter and scoped discovery
Confirm carve-out perimeter, 50/50 JV assumptions, and decision rights
Task.Notes: Resources: Legal + EY + Robert Bosch China Leadership + Alibaba Leadership | Combination model remains the formal carve-out model; the 50/50 JV is the post-close governance construct.

Task.Name: Launch antitrust due diligence tracker, legal workplan, and planning assumptions log
Task.Notes: Resources: Legal + EY | Legal uncertainty is treated as a hard planning dependency, not only a risk note.

Task.Name: Inventory 5 sites, 250 users, 20 applications, and infrastructure service towers
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Robert Bosch China Infrastructure + Alibaba IT | Scope is pure infrastructure services with no SAP in scope.

Task.Name: Define discovery guardrails and phased stakeholder expansion path
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Legal + HR + Communications | Broader validation remains constrained until legal confirms what can be disclosed.

Task.Name: QG0 - Mobilization approved
Task.Notes: Resources: Steering Committee | Restricted-planning controls active, perimeter defined, and initial inventory complete.

Task.Name: Phase 1 - Concept and target-state design
Task.Notes: Operating model, infrastructure target state, migration design, and formal readiness criteria.

Task.Name: 1.1 Operating model and transition principles
Define Day 1 and Day 2 scope boundaries for the JV operating model
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Robert Bosch China IT + Alibaba IT | Scope keeps Day 1 focused on infrastructure continuity and only the 20 in-scope applications.

Task.Name: Define interim service continuity, TSA needs, and exit conditions
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Robert Bosch China IT + Alibaba IT | Seller support is defined only where required until the new JV services are stable.

Task.Name: Confirm sign-off route and approval process under legal confidentiality constraints
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Legal + Steering Committee | Approvals use a narrow escalation route because only limited associates can participate.

Task.Name: 1.2 Infrastructure and security target design
Design target hosting, network, connectivity, and environment segregation
Task.Notes: Resources: Robert Bosch China Infrastructure + Alibaba Cloud + EY | Target state is infrastructure-led and excludes SAP-related architecture.

Task.Name: Design identity, access, endpoint, and security operating model
Task.Notes: Resources: Robert Bosch China Security + Alibaba Security + EY | Identity and access design supports joint governance without broadening access prematurely.

Task.Name: Design collaboration, service management, monitoring, and support stack
Task.Notes: Resources: Robert Bosch China Infrastructure + Alibaba ITSM + EY | Service desk, monitoring, backup, and collaboration capabilities are defined for Day 1 operations.

Task.Name: 1.3 Application and migration design
Classify 20 applications and decide separation pattern for each
Task.Notes: Resources: Application Owners + EY | Each application is classified for retain, reconfigure, replace, or retire within the JV context.

Task.Name: Define migration waves for applications, users, devices, and sites
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Deployment Lead + Infrastructure Leads | Wave design is modest because the scope is 5 sites, 250 users, and 20 applications.

Task.Name: Define test strategy, cutover principles, and rollback approach
Task.Notes: Resources: EY Test Lead + Infrastructure Leads | Rollback and sign-off mechanics are formalized early due to legal and confidentiality sensitivity.

Task.Name: Baseline risk register, dependency log, and entry criteria for build
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Workstream Leads | Gamma-specific risks and dependencies are captured without borrowing reference-project content.

Task.Name: QG1 - Concept approved
Task.Notes: Resources: Steering Committee | Target-state concept, migration design, and restricted approval path are confirmed.

Task.Name: Phase 2 - Build and integrated testing
Task.Notes: Build core services, prepare the 20-application transition set, and complete integrated testing before final readiness.

Task.Name: 2.1 Core infrastructure build
Build landing zone, hosting, and network segmentation
Task.Notes: Resources: Robert Bosch China Infrastructure + Alibaba Cloud | Core environment for the JV is established first to unblock downstream migration tasks.

Task.Name: Build identity, IAM, endpoint, and access services
Task.Notes: Resources: Robert Bosch China Security + Alibaba Security | Identity controls are implemented with least-privilege access due to the restricted stakeholder model.

Task.Name: Build collaboration, service desk, monitoring, and backup services
Task.Notes: Resources: Infrastructure Operations + ITSM Leads | Day 1 support tooling and operational monitoring are completed before system integration testing.

Task.Name: 2.2 Application and data separation build
Reconfigure 20 applications for JV target services
Task.Notes: Resources: Application Owners + Infrastructure Leads | Application work remains intentionally lean because the scope is limited and non-SAP.

Task.Name: Prepare shared data and file separation plus access remediation
Task.Notes: Resources: Data Leads + Security Leads | Shared repositories and access rights are remediated before formal testing starts.

Task.Name: Complete third-party contract, DNS, certificate, and supplier changes
Task.Notes: Resources: Procurement + Legal + Infrastructure Leads | Third-party dependencies are aligned with the future JV operating model and confidentiality constraints.

Task.Name: 2.3 Testing and migration rehearsal
Execute system integration testing across infrastructure services and 20 applications
Task.Notes: Resources: Test Lead + Infrastructure Leads + Application Owners | Integrated validation confirms that the infrastructure-led design supports the limited application set.

Task.Name: Execute UAT with restricted business representatives and clean-team approvers
Task.Notes: Resources: Business Key Users + EY | UAT uses the limited approved representative group because broad awareness is still constrained.

Task.Name: Run cutover rehearsal and rollback validation
Task.Notes: Resources: Infrastructure Leads + EY | Rollback evidence is required before QG2 and QG3 can be passed.

Task.Name: Complete site, device, user, and communications migration packages
Task.Notes: Resources: Deployment Lead + HR + Communications | All migration packages are completed before QG2 and QG3 and before the QG4 readiness window.

Task.Name: QG2 and QG3 - Build and test approved
Task.Notes: Resources: Steering Committee | Build complete, integrated testing passed, and final readiness activities may begin.

Task.Name: Phase 3 - Final readiness and GoLive
Task.Notes: Complete residual pre-GoLive work, hold QG4, run the mandatory final readiness window, and execute Day 1 cutover.

Task.Name: 3.1 Pre-GoLive completion
Close UAT defects and QG2 and QG3 action items
Task.Notes: Resources: Test Lead + Application Owners + Infrastructure Leads | All high-priority defects must be closed before the pre-GoLive gate.

Task.Name: Complete pre-GoLive user, device, and site transitions
Task.Notes: Resources: Deployment Lead + Infrastructure Operations | All migrations are completed before QG4 in line with the schedule rule set.

Task.Name: Complete final security, operational readiness, and support staffing check
Task.Notes: Resources: Security Leads + IT Operations + EY | No critical alerts, unresolved access gaps, or support staffing issues may remain at gate review.

Task.Name: QG4 - Pre-GoLive approved
Task.Notes: Resources: Steering Committee | GoLive is not on the same date as QG4; a mandatory final readiness window follows.

Task.Name: 3.2 Final readiness and open item closure
Verify all UAT defects and QG4 action items are closed
Task.Notes: Resources: Test Lead + PMO | This is the first mandatory final-readiness activity after QG4.

Task.Name: Run final infrastructure and service readiness checks
Task.Notes: Resources: Infrastructure Operations + Security Leads | Readiness checks confirm stability, no critical alerts, and support handoff readiness.

Task.Name: Execute final rollback confirmation and secure business sign-off
Task.Notes: Resources: EY + Business Leads + Infrastructure Leads | Final approval closes the five-day readiness period between QG4 and GoLive.

Task.Name: GoLive - Day 1 cutover complete
Task.Notes: Resources: Steering Committee + Infrastructure Leads | The Gamma JV environment goes live for 5 sites, 250 users, and 20 applications on the committed date.

Task.Name: Phase 4 - Hypercare and closure
Task.Notes: Ninety-day stabilization only after GoLive, followed by formal handover and closure.

Task.Name: 4.1 Hypercare stabilization only
Run 24x7 incident triage and L3 support
Task.Notes: Resources: IT Operations + Service Desk | Hypercare is stabilization only and lasts 90 calendar days.

Task.Name: Operate hotfix-only stabilization for infrastructure and applications
Task.Notes: Resources: Infrastructure Leads + Application Owners | Bug fixes only after GoLive; no new development, migration, or architecture change is allowed.

Task.Name: Deliver user enablement, knowledge transfer, and adoption support
Task.Notes: Resources: Training Lead + Service Desk + PMO | Support focuses on stabilizing the new operating model for the restricted but growing user community.

Task.Name: 4.2 Transition service exit and steady-state handover
Retire residual seller-hosted services and confirm exit criteria
Task.Notes: Resources: Robert Bosch China IT + Alibaba IT + EY | Only residual transition services remain after GoLive and are retired in a controlled manner.

Task.Name: Finalize JV runbooks, supplier handoffs, and service ownership
Task.Notes: Resources: IT Operations + Procurement + PMO | Operational ownership is formalized for the jointly managed JV steady state.

Task.Name: Confirm operational KPIs and steady-state governance handover
Task.Notes: Resources: Steering Committee + IT Operations + EY | Readiness for steady-state operations is confirmed after hypercare evidence and service exits are complete.

Task.Name: QG5 - Project completion approved
Task.Notes: Resources: Steering Committee | Ninety-day hypercare is complete, transition services are exited, and operational handover is accepted.

Task.Name: 4.3 Project closure
Capture lessons learned and archive project artefacts
Task.Notes: Resources: PMO + Workstream Leads | Repository artefacts and handover evidence are archived after QG5 approval.

Task.Name: Close financials, confirm documentation, and issue closure report
Task.Notes: Resources: Finance + PMO | Closure report finalizes the initial v1.0 Gamma package baseline.

Task.Name: Closure - Gamma programme archived
Task.Notes: Resources: Steering Committee | Formal closure milestone aligned with the committed completion date.
